patch.value: CodeFactor remediation: drive code quality from A to A+ (zero outstanding findings)

Track and eliminate every CodeFactor finding so the repository reaches the best attainable rating with no outstanding issues.

## Source

- CodeFactor overview: https://www.codefactor.io/repository/github/unbraind/pm-cli/overview/main
- Issues: https://www.codefactor.io/repository/github/unbraind/pm-cli/issues/main
- Files by rating: https://www.codefactor.io/repository/github/unbraind/pm-cli/files/main?grid-column=Rating&grid-dir=0

CodeFactor was wired up alongside Codecov in commit `d42ae71b` (README badge + `.codefactor.yml` scoping analysis to hand-authored source, excluding `.agents/**`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `PRD.md`, `pnpm-lock.yaml`).

## Snapshot (captured 2026-06-26)

- Overall grade: **A** (98.7% A-rated files, 1.0% B, 0.3% C)
- Files analyzed: 601 · Active branch: main
- **Total outstanding issues: 261**

| Category | Count | Severity / rule split |
|---|---|---|
| Complexity | 152 | Critical 3 · High 7 · Moderate 142 |
| Maintainability | 89 | `no-useless-fallback-in-spread` 57 · unsafe optional chaining 25 · `no-thenable` 4 · `no-useless-spread` 2 · `no-useless-length-check` 1 |
| Duplication | 20 | Critical 2 · Major 3 · Minor 15 |

## Hotspot files (lowest-rated)

1. `src/sdk/cli-contracts.ts` (B) — includes the `no-thenable` findings (`then:` keys at lines 2483, 2493)
2. `tests/unit/commands/create-command.spec.ts` (C)
3. `tests/unit/commands/update-command.spec.ts` (B)
4. `src/cli/commands/search.ts` (B)
5. `src/cli/main.ts` (B)

## Approach

Child items break the work down by category and rule. Order of attack:
1. Maintainability — mechanical, low-risk, auto-fixable ESLint rules (fastest grade wins).
2. Duplication — extract shared helpers (Critical/Major first).
3. Complexity — refactor the worst functions (Critical/High first), then chip away at Moderate.
4. Stand up local lint/quality parity so the team can reach zero and prevent regressions.

NOTE: CodeFactor analyzes its own snapshot; `.agents/**` (the git-native work-item store, where these very items live), `CHANGELOG.md`, `PRD.md`, and the lockfile are excluded by `.codefactor.yml`, so these tracking items do not themselves add findings.

CodeFactor overall repository grade reaches A+ (or the highest attainable) on branch main; CodeFactor reports 0 outstanding issues across Complexity, Maintainability, and Duplication; local lint/quality parity tooling exists so findings can be reproduced and kept at zero (no regressions on new PRs).

value.author: codex-gpt5
value.text: Progress update: live CodeFactor for main still reports 255 issues until this branch lands (83 maintainability, 152 complexity, 20 duplication). This branch completes the maintainability groups tracked by pm-xsth, pm-m8yl, and pm-x878/Codecov; complexity pm-arzz and duplication pm-fmjy remain open for dedicated refactor follow-up.

value.author: codex-codefactor-sdk-cli-20260626
value.text: Investigation note: live 2026-06-26 CodeFactor public issues page reports A rating, 601 analyzed files, 172 issues after the merged maintainability branch; PM snapshot still tracks remaining complexity and duplication children. GitHub state: no open PRs, no open Dependabot or code-scanning alerts; issue #374 remains open and externally blocked on missing RELEASE_PAT in release environment; pnpm audit --audit-level moderate reports no known vulnerabilities. Dependency drift from pnpm outdated: @sentry/cli 3.5.1 -> 3.6.0, @sentry/node 10.61.0 -> 10.62.0, @types/node held at Node 22 types despite latest 26.x.

value.text: Review-fix evidence: addressed Cubic P2 findings on PR #389 by scoping shared runtime --unset resolution to command_to_fields for create/update and by rejecting malformed non-string legacy none/null collection entries with a usage PmCliError. Updated helper fixtures to model command_to_fields and added a malformed payload regression test. Verification after fix passed: focused create/update/legacy-none suite (3 files, 214 tests), legacy-none focused suite (11 tests), pnpm typecheck, pnpm quality:static, pnpm lint, git diff --check, and full coverage 256 files / 5138 tests / 100/100/100/100.

value.author: codex-20260627-sdk-cli
value.text: Review evidence: addressed Gemini current-head feedback by hardening runtime unset field resolution against malformed dynamic registry fields. resolveRuntimeUnsetFieldDefinition now ignores missing/blank/non-string key, metadata_key, cli_flag, and cli_aliases entries before matching unset tokens; create-command tests cover malformed array and non-array alias metadata. Verification after this fix: node scripts/run-tests.mjs test -- tests/unit/commands/create-command.spec.ts tests/unit/commands/update-command.spec.ts tests/unit/commands/legacy-none-tokens.spec.ts passed 3 files / 215 tests; pnpm typecheck passed; pnpm quality:static passed; node scripts/run-tests.mjs coverage passed 256 files / 5138 tests with 100/100/100/100 coverage; pnpm lint passed; git diff --check passed.

value.author: codex-20260627-sdk-cli
value.text: Review-fix evidence: addressed current-head Cubic finding, also echoed by an older CodeRabbit thread, by normalizing runtime unset lookup tokens and all runtime field candidate forms before matching. This preserves camelCase key lookup for dynamically registered fields such as githubUrl after create/update lowercases --unset input, while keeping the malformed registry guards from the prior fix. Added create-command regression coverage for the lowercased githuburl key path. Verification after fix passed: focused create/update/legacy-none suite (3 files, 215 tests), pnpm typecheck, pnpm quality:static, node scripts/run-tests.mjs coverage (256 files, 5138 tests, 100/100/100/100), pnpm lint, and git diff --check.

value.author: codex-gpt5
value.text: Review fix evidence for PR #391: addressed the Gemini Code Assist lifecycle finding by changing generated imperative and declarative package starter tests to use createExtensionTestHarness-bound assert/run helpers consistently and to call await ext.deactivate() in finally blocks for every activation-bearing sample. Verification after the fix: node scripts/run-tests.mjs test -- tests/unit/extensions/extension-scaffold-define-guidance.spec.ts passed 26/26; node scripts/run-tests.mjs test -- tests/unit/extensions/extension-command.spec.ts passed 111/111; pnpm typecheck passed; pnpm quality:static passed; pnpm lint passed; git diff --check passed; pnpm build passed; node scripts/run-tests.mjs coverage passed 256 files / 5138 tests with 100% statements, 100% branches, 100% functions, 100% lines; rebuilt manual temp smoke at /tmp/tmp.fUHNts0IWX passed pm init, package init, install, package doctor ok with zero warnings, starter command JSON invocation, generated package npm run typecheck, and generated package npm test 2/2; pnpm contracts:check passed; pnpm security:scan reported no credential-like secrets; pnpm audit --audit-level moderate reported no known vulnerabilities; node scripts/release/sentry-telemetry-gate.mjs --json --telemetry-mode best-effort passed with Sentry critical=0 high=0 total=0 and telemetry finish_error_rate_pct=0.75 failures_without_error_code_rows=0; pnpm changelog:pm:check used pm-changelog 2026.6.27 and left CHANGELOG.md unchanged at item_count=1363; pm test pm-92if --run --progress passed all 10 linked tests.

value.author: codex-gpt5
value.text: Second review fix evidence for PR #391: addressed current-head Gemini, CodeRabbit, and cubic feedback by making generated starter tests assert ext.deactivate() results with assertExtensionDeactivated(teardown) at the end of successful try blocks, while keeping finally blocks for fallback cleanup only via if (!deactivated) await ext.deactivate(); this avoids masking primary assertion failures while still detecting teardown regressions. Also tightened scaffold tests to assert the generated try/finally cleanup shape and teardown assertion. Verification after this fix: node scripts/run-tests.mjs test -- tests/unit/extensions/extension-command.spec.ts passed 111/111; node scripts/run-tests.mjs test -- tests/unit/extensions/extension-scaffold-define-guidance.spec.ts passed 26/26; pnpm typecheck passed; pnpm lint passed; pnpm quality:static passed; git diff --check passed; node scripts/run-tests.mjs coverage passed 256 files / 5138 tests with 100% statements, 100% branches, 100% functions, and 100% lines; rebuilt manual temp smoke at /tmp/tmp.vWMdOkZsal passed pm init, package init, install, package doctor ok with zero warnings, starter command JSON invocation, generated package npm run typecheck, and generated package npm test 2/2; pnpm contracts:check passed; pnpm security:scan reported no credential-like secrets; pnpm audit --audit-level moderate reported no known vulnerabilities; Sentry/telemetry gate passed with critical=0 high=0 total=0 and telemetry finish_error_rate_pct=0.75 failures_without_error_code_rows=0; pnpm changelog:pm:check used pm-changelog 2026.6.27 and left CHANGELOG.md unchanged at item_count=1363; pm test pm-92if --run --progress passed all 10 linked tests.

value.author: codex-gpt5
value.text: Third PR #391 review fix evidence: addressed current-head Gemini and cubic feedback by wrapping generated fallback ext.deactivate() cleanup calls in try/catch so cleanup cannot mask the primary assertion or teardown failure. Added scaffold expectations for let deactivated = false, assertExtensionDeactivated(teardown), guarded fallback cleanup, and catch {} in command, non-command, and declarative starter test templates. Verification after this fix: pnpm build; focused extension-command and extension-scaffold tests; pnpm typecheck; git diff --check; pnpm quality:static; pnpm contracts:check; pnpm security:scan; pnpm lint; full coverage 256 files / 5138 tests / 100% statements, branches, functions, lines; pnpm audit --audit-level moderate no known vulnerabilities; sentry telemetry gate ok with 0 critical/high Sentry issues and telemetry finish error rate 0.75%; pnpm changelog:pm:check with pm-changelog 2026.6.27 unchanged CHANGELOG; manual temp smoke at /tmp/tmp.wdDfYPF1gm covered init, package init, install, package doctor ok with 0 warnings, starter command ping, generated package typecheck, and generated package tests; pm test pm-92if --run --progress passed all 10 linked tests.

value.text: Review-fix evidence for PR #407 current head: addressed Greptile P2 starter-prefixed reserved-root collision feedback by disambiguating starter-* package command paths only when the following word is a reserved pm root, while preserving readable non-reserved names such as starter-tool-kit. Verification: extension scaffold guidance 28/28, extension command runtime 113/113, pnpm typecheck, pnpm lint, git diff --check, manual temp project smoke with view-kit/starter-package/starter-tool-kit package init/install/invoke/package doctor, and node scripts/run-tests.mjs coverage with 5259/5259 tests and 100/100/100/100 coverage.

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Final Greptile edge-case evidence for PR #407: addressed residual starter-starter-* collision by treating starter itself as a reserved scaffold command root and simplifying buildScaffoldCommandName to one reserved-root prefix rule. This makes starter-search dispatch as starter starter search ping and starter-starter-search dispatch as starter starter starter search ping, avoiding duplicate command registration. Verification after this fix passed: scaffold guidance tests 28/28, extension-command runtime tests 113/113, pnpm typecheck, pnpm lint/static-quality, git diff --check, isolated temp smoke at /tmp/pm-scaffold-review3-smoke-H9GcmG with view-kit/starter-search/starter-starter-search package init/npm install/npm test/package install/command invocation/package doctor, and node scripts/run-tests.mjs coverage with 5259/5259 tests and 100/100/100/100 coverage.

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Review follow-up evidence: addressed current-head scaffold review feedback by lowercasing reserved command roots at initialization and narrowing starter-prefixed collision handling. Non-reserved starter package names such as starter-tool-kit now remain readable as 'starter tool kit ping', while reserved descendants such as starter-package, starter-search, and starter-starter-search keep an added starter prefix because package/search are existing pm command roots and starter-only names would otherwise collide with the fallback prefix. Verification after this change: node scripts/run-tests.mjs test -- tests/unit/extensions/extension-scaffold-define-guidance.spec.ts (28/28), node scripts/run-tests.mjs test -- tests/unit/extensions/extension-command.spec.ts (113/113), pnpm typecheck, pnpm lint, git diff --check, manual temp smoke at /tmp/pm-scaffold-review4-smoke-IbixgD for starter-package/starter-search/starter-starter-search/starter-tool-kit generated install invoke doctor flows, and node scripts/run-tests.mjs coverage (262 files, 5259 tests, 100% statements/branches/functions/lines: 32128/32128 statements, 24854/24854 branches, 5510/5510 functions, 30832/30832 lines).
